doc: Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_overflow (mpfr/src/exceptions.c L424-L448): construct the overflow-exception value MPFR's rounding logic returns when a computation overflows. Given (rnd, sign), branches on MPFR_IS_LIKE_RNDZ(rnd, sign<0) to choose between ±max-finite (toward-zero branch, ternary=-1 in C with sign-multiplication) and ±Inf (away-from-zero branch, ternary=+1). The TS port returns a fresh Result {value, ternary} per the locked schema; the C side mutates x and returns the ternary directly. Class is 'misc' for the 1000ms budget; the actual work is two enum lookups plus a delegate call to mpfr_setmax or the posInf/negInf factory. The default emax is MPFR_EMAX_DEFAULT = 2^30 - 1 (mpfr/src/mpfr.h L231), matching libmpfr's default __gmpfr_emax. No global flag side effects are modelled — pure functions only.
c_signature: int mpfr_overflow(mpfr_ptr x, mpfr_rnd_t rnd_mode, int sign)
divergence_from_c: C takes (mpfr_ptr x, mpfr_rnd_t rnd_mode, int sign): x is mutated to ±max-finite or ±Inf and the ternary is returned as int. TS takes (prec, rnd, sign): a fresh Result is returned. Sign is strictly typed in TS as 1 | -1 (the C side accepts int and asserts non-zero via MPFR_ASSERT_SIGN — the TS equivalent is throw-on-non-±1). The TS port does NOT set libmpfr's __gmpfr_flags (no global flag state in the locked schema). The C signature receives the precision implicitly via x; the TS port takes prec explicitly because the returned value carries it (posInf/negInf require prec). The C side uses __gmpfr_emax which is configurable via mpfr_set_emax; the TS port hard-codes the default MPFR_EMAX_DEFAULT = 2^30 - 1 because no current library function exposes emax management — callers that have changed emax would not match this golden.
refs: mpfr/src/exceptions.c L424-L448 — the C reference body.
src/ops/setmax.ts — delegate for the max-finite branch.
src/ops/underflow.ts — sibling exception constructor (mirror image; same structure, ±0 / ±min instead of ±max / ±Inf).
src/core.ts L137-L151 — RoundingMode (5 modes, no RNDF).
CLAUDE.md 'Hallucination-risk callouts: Ternary flag is the sign of (rounded - exact)' — direction is critical.
